Description:
Set up the FastAPI application entrypoint, Pydantic settings, structured JSON logging, domain models, keyword filtering, and in-memory fake database for MVP 1.

SCOPE: MVP 1
PROJECT AREA: Backend
STORIES: 2.1 - 2.6

Create backend/main.py as the FastAPI application entrypoint with agent auto-discovery.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Instantiate FastAPI app with title, version, and description
- Mount router with /api/v1/ prefix: include jobs router, scrape router
- Add /health endpoint returning {status: ok, version: x.x.x}
- Import all agent modules for auto-registration (linkedin, jobserve)
- Add startup event logging app ready message via get_logger
- Configure CORS middleware for local dev (allow localhost:3000)

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-1

TESTING CRITERIA:
- uvicorn starts without errors
- GET /health returns 200 with JSON body
- OpenAPI docs available at /docs
- Agent imports trigger @register decorators

EDGE CASES:
- Import order matters — agent imports must come after registry module is loaded

Create backend/settings.py with Pydantic Settings class for all typed environment variables.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Inherit from pydantic_settings.BaseSettings
- Define all env vars as typed fields: SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, DATABASE_URL, OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, REDIS_URL, TEMPORAL_SERVER_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL
- Set defaults where safe; mark required fields with no default
- Instantiate settings singleton at module level: settings = Settings()
- App fails at startup if any required var is missing (Pydantic validates on init)
- Export settings from module for import by all other modules

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-13

TESTING CRITERIA:
- App startup fails with clear error if required env var is missing
- All vars accessible via settings.field_name
- .env.example matches all fields defined in Settings

EDGE CASES:
- Optional vars (MVP 2+ only) must have defaults to avoid breaking MVP 1 startup

Create backend/logging_config.py with shared JSONFormatter and get_logger() function.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Define JSONFormatter(logging.Formatter) with format() method
- format() returns json.dumps({level, agent, message, timestamp})
- Define get_logger(name: str) -> logging.Logger
- Attach StreamHandler(sys.stdout) with JSONFormatter to logger
- Set log level to INFO
- All agent and router modules import get_logger — zero print() calls anywhere
- Add module-level logger in main.py using get_logger('main')

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-13

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Log output is valid JSON on every line
- level, agent, message, timestamp fields always present
- No print() calls exist anywhere in backend/

EDGE CASES:
- Avoid duplicate handlers if get_logger() called multiple times for same name

Create backend/models.py with Job and ScrapeResult Pydantic schemas for MVP 1.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Job model: id (UUID), title (str), company (str), location (str), source (str — source_id from agent), url (str), description (str), relevance_score (float | None = None), scraped_at (datetime)
- ScrapeResult model: results (dict[str, int] — {source_id: count}), total_inserted (int), duration_seconds (float)
- JobListResponse model: total (int), page (int), page_size (int), has_next (bool), next_cursor (str | None), linkedin_count (int), jobserve_count (int), jobs (list[Job])
- All models use Pydantic v2 syntax (model_config, not class Config)
- Add JSON serialisation config for datetime fields

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-13

TESTING CRITERIA:
- All fields typed correctly
- Pydantic v2 validation works (no v1 compat warnings)
- JobListResponse used in GET /api/v1/jobs response

EDGE CASES:
- UUID fields must serialise to string in JSON responses

Create backend/filters.py with title and description substring matching for relevance pre-filtering.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Define KEYWORDS list: senior, lead, principal, architect, full-stack, fullstack, backend, frontend, AI, ML, LLM, FastAPI, Next.js, TypeScript, Python, Azure, AWS, contract
- Define filter_jobs(jobs: list[Job], keywords: list[str] | None = None) -> list[Job]
- Match case-insensitively against job.title and job.description
- If no keywords provided, use default KEYWORDS list
- Return only jobs matching at least one keyword
- Log filtered count via get_logger

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-16

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Jobs with no keyword matches excluded
- Case-insensitive matching works
- Custom keyword list overrides defaults
- Empty result set handled gracefully

EDGE CASES:
- Jobs with empty description field must not crash filter
- Unicode characters in titles handled correctly

Implement dict-backed in-memory store in main.py as explicit MVP 1 exception before Supabase.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Define fake_db: dict[str, Job] = {} at module level in main.py
- Expose helper functions: add_job(job: Job), get_jobs() -> list[Job], get_job(id: str) -> Job | None, clear_jobs()
- All scraper agents write to fake_db via add_job()
- Jobs router reads from fake_db via get_jobs() and get_job()
- Add comment: # MVP 1 ONLY — replaced by Supabase asyncpg in MVP 2 (Story 8.1)
- Deduplication by job URL: skip if job.url already in fake_db values

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-16

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Scrape triggers add jobs to fake_db
- GET /api/v1/jobs returns jobs from fake_db
- Duplicate URLs are not inserted twice
- clear_jobs() empties the store

EDGE CASES:
- Thread safety: fake_db not safe for concurrent writes — acceptable for MVP 1 single-worker local use only

Build the pluggable scraper registry with BaseScrapeAgent ABC, @register decorator, LinkedIn and JobServe agents, and registry-driven scrape router.

SCOPE: MVP 1
PROJECT AREA: Backend / Agents
STORIES: 3.1 - 3.5

Create backend/agents/base.py defining the abstract base class all scraper agents must implement.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Define BaseScrapeAgent(ABC) with three abstract members:
  - @property source_id -> str (unique stable identifier e.g. linkedin)
  - @property display_name -> str (human-readable for dashboard)
  - async run() -> int (executes scrape; returns count of new jobs inserted)
- Add docstrings explaining contract for each abstract member
- Import and use get_logger in base class for shared logging behaviour
- Do NOT import any specific agent — base has no knowledge of implementations

EFFORT: S (1 hour)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-15

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Instantiating BaseScrapeAgent directly raises TypeError
- Subclass without all three members raises TypeError
- Valid subclass instantiates and runs correctly

EDGE CASES:
- source_id must be URL-safe (lowercase, hyphens only — validate in __init_subclass__)

Create backend/agents/registry.py with @register decorator and agent discovery functions.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Define _REGISTRY: dict[str, Type[BaseScrapeAgent]] = {} at module level
- Define @register decorator: instantiates agent, stores class in _REGISTRY by source_id, returns class unchanged
- Define get_all_agents() -> list[BaseScrapeAgent]: returns [cls() for cls in _REGISTRY.values()]
- Define get_agent(source_id: str) -> BaseScrapeAgent | None: returns instantiated agent or None
- Log registration event per agent via get_logger
- Guard against duplicate source_id registration with ValueError

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-19

TESTING CRITERIA:
- @register on a valid subclass adds it to _REGISTRY
- Duplicate source_id raises ValueError
- get_all_agents() returns one instance per registered agent
- get_agent('unknown') returns None

EDGE CASES:
- Registry must be importable before any agent module imports to avoid circular imports

Create backend/agents/linkedin/linkedin_agent.py implementing BaseScrapeAgent with Playwright.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Apply @register decorator; source_id='linkedin'; display_name='LinkedIn'
- Use Playwright async API with Chromium in headless mode
- Search URL: https://www.linkedin.com/jobs/search/?keywords={query}&location=United+Kingdom
- Randomise delays between actions: asyncio.sleep(random.uniform(2, 5))
- Extract: title, company, location, url, description for each listing
- Deduplicate by URL before inserting to fake_db
- Implement anti-bot: randomise user-agent, randomise viewport, slow-type search terms
- Close browser in finally block; register SIGTERM handler
- Return count of new jobs inserted

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend / Agents
DEPENDENCIES: JD-20, JD-18

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Agent registers with source_id='linkedin' on import
- Playwright Chromium launches without errors
- Jobs returned match keyword filter
- Duplicate URLs not re-inserted
- SIGTERM closes browser cleanly

EDGE CASES:
- LinkedIn login wall: handle gracefully with logged warning, return 0
- Rate limiting / CAPTCHA: log and return 0 (never bypass)
- Selector changes: wrap in try/except with field-level fallback

Create backend/agents/jobserve/jobserve_agent.py implementing BaseScrapeAgent with Playwright.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Apply @register decorator; source_id='jobserve'; display_name='JobServe'
- Search URL: https://www.jobserve.com/gb/en/JobSearch.aspx?shid={query}
- Randomise pagination: click next page with random delay (1.5 - 4s)
- Extract: title, company, location, url, description per listing
- Normalise location field (strip trailing whitespace, UK postcode handling)
- Deduplicate by URL before inserting to fake_db
- Register SIGTERM handler; close browser in finally block
- Return count of new jobs inserted

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend / Agents
DEPENDENCIES: JD-20, JD-18

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Agent registers with source_id='jobserve' on import
- Pagination handled correctly
- Data normalised consistently
- Duplicate URLs not re-inserted

EDGE CASES:
- JobServe pagination may use session cookies — handle cookie jar
- Empty search results page must not crash agent

Create backend/routers/scrape.py with registry-driven POST /api/v1/scrape endpoint.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Import get_all_agents from agents.registry
- Define APIRouter with prefix /api/v1 and tag scrape
- POST /scrape endpoint: iterate get_all_agents(), call agent.run(), collect results dict
- Return ScrapeResult with results, total_inserted, duration_seconds
- Log start and completion of each agent via get_logger
- No hardcoded source names anywhere in router

EFFORT: S (2 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: JD-20, JD-16

TESTING CRITERIA:
- POST /api/v1/scrape returns ScrapeResult JSON
- results dict contains entry per registered agent
- Adding new agent requires zero router changes
- duration_seconds reflects actual wall-clock time

EDGE CASES:
- One agent failure must not prevent other agents from running (wrap each agent.run() in try/except)
